summary.primary_use: Use this index to turn ACT/Goods wiki context into GrantScope search terms, source expansion, project pipeline actions, and application evidence packs.

_meta.description: ACT Unified Project Codes - Use these codes across ALL systems

tier_descriptions.ecosystem: The 5 core ACT ecosystem projects — these are the platforms
tier_descriptions.studio: Art, storytelling, and creative projects that feed life into the ecosystem — if art isn't being made and stories aren't being told, the whole system dies
tier_descriptions.satellite: Supporting projects, partnerships, and community initiatives

projects.name: Goods on Country
projects.summary: Practical enterprise lane for remote-community essential goods, circular materials, asset tracking, procurement, grants, and impact investment.

source_documents.label: Goods on Country Path to Thousands 2026

evidence.label: Tracked deployment proof
evidence.detail: 404 current CSV rows and 389 QR manifest rows were found locally; reconcile CSV growth against the 389-asset canonical claim before external use.

evidence.label: Product mix
evidence.value: 363 Basket Bed, 21 Weave Bed, 20 ID Washing Machine
evidence.detail: Use product mix to prove real delivery and to forecast replacement, production, freight, and support needs.

evidence.label: Community footprint
evidence.detail: Tennant Creek (146), Palm Island (141), Utopia Homelands (68), Maningrida (24), Kalgoorlie (20), Mount Isa (2)

evidence.label: Operating system
evidence.detail: ACT repo map links Goods to ACT-GD and the Goods production URL.

routes.label: Remote housing and essential-goods buyers
routes.why: Goods already has deployed assets, demand proof, QR support infrastructure, and a 2026 scale plan that names housing, hostels, retail, ACCHO, and community-housing routes.
routes.next_action: Create one procurement pack: unit costs, minimum order quantities, delivery model, warranty/support, impact proof, and buyer-specific ask.

routes.label: Health, housing, First Nations, circular economy foundations
routes.why: The strongest foundation case is not generic product funding; it is the link between remote housing, dignity, laundry/health outcomes, circular materials, First Nations production, and community ownership.
routes.next_action: Build a foundation narrative pack with the Catalysing Impact draft, asset evidence, community demand, and a clear repayable/non-repayable funding split.

routes.label: Open grants for products, capability, evidence, and regional delivery
routes.why: GrantScope should search beyond research grants: manufacturing capability, circular economy, Indigenous business, regional health, climate resilience, social enterprise, logistics, and community infrastructure.
routes.next_action: Use the Goods feed first, then add missing sources to the public-source frontier when the match is real and public.

routes.label: Working capital and impact investment
routes.why: The current evidence supports a capital stack: pre-purchase contracts, repayable finance, R&D tax, catalytic grants, and a First Nations-owned operating pathway.
routes.next_action: Turn the 2026 plan into a capital table: ask, source, repayment logic, evidence needed, and owner/date for each route.

routes.label: Application proof and impact measurement
routes.why: The strongest application material is already split across the asset register, QR manifest, community demand, Catalysing Impact draft, and ACT project-code evidence.
routes.next_action: Create a repeatable evidence pack: facts, source document, proof status, and where it appears in applications.

readiness_gaps: Reconcile the canonical asset count against the latest expanded CSV before sending external claims.
Confirm the preferred legal/entity pathway for IPP and Supply Nation positioning.
Create a buyer-ready unit economics sheet for beds, washing machines, freight, warranty, and support.
Separate grants, procurement revenue, pre-purchase contracts, loans, and R&D tax in the capital stack.
Turn community demand and health evidence into reusable application language.

projects.name: CivicGraph / GrantScope
projects.summary: Infrastructure lane for funding intelligence, source discovery, public data, grant/foundation matching, and relationship evidence.

evidence.label: Repo linked
evidence.detail: GrantScope is the support layer for finding, scoring, tracking, and sending opportunities into CRM/pipeline workflows.

routes.label: Public-source grant discovery
routes.why: Use GrantScope to search public grant sources and convert strong matches into tracked pipeline, not to make users search from scratch.
routes.next_action: Keep expanding the public source frontier and classify sources by state, source family, project fit, and readiness.

routes.label: GHL and pipeline handoff
routes.why: The value is in moving real opportunities into contacts, tasks, and follow-up, not just listing grants.
routes.next_action: Use scored matches to create tasks, contact records, and follow-up notes for the right ACT project lane.

readiness_gaps: Make source quality visible so users know what is ready, stale, local, national, or needs verification.

support_actions.title: Run GrantScope grant scan
support_actions.next_step: Keep expanding the public source frontier and classify sources by state, source family, project fit, and readiness.

projects.name: JusticeHub
projects.summary: Justice-system change lane for evidence, youth justice, diversion, reintegration, community support, and policy/practice alignment.

routes.label: Youth justice and reintegration grants
routes.why: The strongest search terms should cover justice reform, diversion, community-led support, family support, and evidence translation.
routes.next_action: Use ALMA/evidence context to distinguish research, implementation, service delivery, and advocacy opportunities.

routes.label: Systems-change foundations
routes.why: JusticeHub needs relationship-led foundation work around justice reform, young people, families, First Nations justice, and systems accountability.
routes.next_action: Create a foundation shortlist with fit, relationship path, proof, and a specific ask per funder.

readiness_gaps: Separate service delivery, advocacy, evidence platform, and policy reform asks before matching funders.

projects.name: Empathy Ledger
projects.summary: Community voice, Indigenous data sovereignty, consent, storytelling, and accountable evidence infrastructure.

routes.next_action: Package Empathy Ledger as the evidence/voice infrastructure that strengthens ACT project applications.

readiness_gaps: Clarify when Empathy Ledger is a standalone product, a partner service, or an embedded evidence layer for another project.
